European Central Bank Looks Set to Cut Growth Outlook Amid Tariffs, Weak Data — Market Talk
data.description: The European Central Bank is likely to set out a gloomier growth prognosis as underlying weakness is exacerbated by trade tensions, Aegon AM's Colin Finlayson writes in a note to clients. The central bank on Thursday will release its latest staff projections for growth and inflation in the eurozone, and is likely to downgrade its current estimates for both, Finlayson says. "This fits with the pattern of recent data releases and the impact of any trade tariffs that are ultimately levied... the ECB is having to deal with data that are already weak," he says. "These forces would only be exacerbated by any U.S. trade tariffs," Finlayson says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

data.title: Italian Mediobanca investors holding 11.9% stake back Banca Generali bid
data.description:
A group of Italian investors in the Italian merchant bank Mediobanca , holding a total stake of 11.9%, broadly backed the proposed bid for private bank Banca Generali in a meeting on Wednesday, two of the shareholders said.

Mediobanca proposed buying Banca Generali in April as it seeks to stave off a buyout bid from Monte dei Paschi di Siena (MPS) .

Due to the MPS bid and Italian corporate takeover rules, Mediobanca must first seek shareholders' approval to pursue the Banca Generali deal and has called a meeting for June 16 to vote on the plan.

The group of Italian investors, which includes the asset manager Banca Mediolanum , signed a consultation agreement in 2018 to share observations on the main topics regarding Mediobanca, with no restrictions on voting at the ordinary and extraordinary general shareholders' meetings.

data.title: DUAE premium volume grew 14.5% in 2024 to $89.9 billion, AM Best report shows
data.description:
(The Insurer) - MGAs and other delegated underwriting authority enterprises (DUAEs) once again booked double-digit growth in 2024, with new figures from AM Best showing the segment generated $89.9 billion in direct premiums written (DPW).

That figure, based solely on data sourced from the U.S. National Association of Insurance Commissioners, was up 14.5% from 2023’s $78.6 billion and marked the fourth consecutive year in which growth exceeded 10%.

AM Best said the increase came about despite average account pricing moderating or even declining in some lines of business, including workers’ compensation, professional liability, D&O, employment practices liability and cyber.

“The premium momentum that is owed to MGAs writing specialty commercial lines of coverage shows no sign of slowing down,” the rating agency said in a market update.

However, the report also said certain market segments are experiencing moderated pricing, which could affect future growth rates, especially for newer or less capitalised MGAs.

At the same time, new market entrants that have knowledge and expertise in niche, highly specialised markets remain attractive to insurers that have both the capacity and appetite to assume specialty risk.

AM Best said its DPW data "does not represent the entire universe of MGAs since many MGAs generate annual premiums for insurance companies that fall below the minimum threshold … required for the MGA’s premium to be reported via Note 19 in the annual NAIC statement".

The rating agency said it counted more than 700 U.S. MGAs that met that threshold in 2024, up from more than 600 in 2023.

However, market research indicates that the total number of MGAs operating in the U.S. market currently totals more than 1,000, AM Best said.

DUAE FOCUS

The number of insurers that generated $500 million or more in DPW through exclusive partnerships with MGAs grew to 19 in 2024, compared with 12 in the previous year. Six booked more than $1 billion, AM Best’s report said, compared with three in 2023. Eighteen of the top 20 grew their DPW in 2024, including 12 with double-digit premium growth.

“While increased premium totals point to general market trends, it also reflects that insurers are directing increased resources and placing a heightened level of strategic importance on their exclusive MGA relationships to help achieve business plan goals,” the agency said.

For the top 20 insurers generating DPW through non-exclusive MGA partnerships, DPW totalled $24.1 billion, which accounted for 26.8% of the total MGA-produced premium for 2024.

In 2024, there were 11 insurers that generated more than $1 billion of DPW through non-exclusive MGA partnerships, up from six the year before.

data.title: iMotion Automotive Technology to Supply Front Camera Product for Vehicles
data.description:
iMotion Automotive Technology has been selected to supply an intelligent front camera (iFC) product to a leading independent brand for nine vehicle models on its commercial vehicle platform, a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The autonomous driving solution company will also provide advanced driver assistance system (ADAS) solutions with the integration of the software and hardware based on Horizon Robotics' Journey 3 chip.

The vehicle models are expected to enter mass production in March 2026.

data.title: EU OKs Madison Dearborn's Purchase of NextGen Healthcare Stake
data.description:
The European Commission approved Madison Dearborn Partners' acquisition of a significant ownership stake in NextGen Healthcare from Thoma Bravo.

The commission found that the deal is not expected to raise competition concerns, according to a Wednesday release.

On May 2, US private equity investor Madison Dearborn announced an agreement to acquire the stake in NextGen, with private equity and growth capital firm Thoma Bravo retaining its shareholding. The deal is anticipated to be completed in the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: France’s 10-Year OAT Yield Steady as Markets Anticipate ECB Cut
data.description:
France’s 10-year OAT yield remained steady at 3.2%, near its lowest point in a month, as investors awaited the European Central Bank’s policy decision on Thursday.

Markets broadly anticipate a 25 basis point rate cut following softer-than-expected Eurozone inflation, with consumer prices rising 1.9% year-on-year in May—below the 2.0% forecast and falling beneath the ECB’s target for the first time since September 2024.

This weaker inflation data has raised expectations of further easing, despite recent hawkish comments from some ECB officials advocating for a pause.

On the trade front, EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefcovic described talks with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer in Paris as “productive and constructive,” noting that discussions are “moving in the right direction,” indicating the EU may secure an exemption from President Donald Trump’s recent decision to double tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to 50%.

data.title: CrowdStrike Shares Decline on 1Q Loss, Outlook
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

CrowdStrike Holdings shares declined 6.7% in premarket trading after the company swung to a loss in its latest quarter and posted a lower-than-expected outlook.

Shares were trading around $456 before the bell. The stock was up 43% on the year as of Tuesday's close.

The cybersecurity company late Tuesday said it swung to a loss of $110.2 million, or 44 cents a share, in the quarter ended April 30, compared with a profit of $42.8 million, or 17 cents a share, a year earlier.

In the current fiscal second quarter, CrowdStrike is expecting to record $1.14 billion to $1.15 billion in revenue, below the $1.16 billion analysts were anticipating.

CrowdStrike said the costs of a widespread software outage last July continue to weigh on results, and that revenue is still being hurt by an incentive program it launched to try to retain customers after that outage.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com
